source.title: Disentangling pleasure from incentive salience and learning signals in brain reward circuitry
source.venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences USA

confidence_rationale: Single-unit recording in ventral pallidum (VP) showing that the SAME neurons encode hedonic value (taste palatability) and incentive salience (cue-evoked motivational firing), and that these two codes are dynamically linked: the hedonic firing pattern at receipt is what causes the cue-evoked wanting pattern to track current physiological state (sodium-depleted vs sated). This is among the cleanest direct electrophysiological evidence that liking provides the input signal that lets a wanting-coding circuit appropriately gate cue-driven approach -- which is the structural claim of MECH-295. Confidence 0.82: high-quality single-unit data, direct mechanistic mapping (palatability shift => cue-firing shift), but the experiment uses a homeostatic-shift paradigm (sodium depletion) rather than a hedonic-blockade paradigm, so the necessity is shown by drive-state-dependent palatability gating wanting, not by direct liking ablation.
failure_signatures: If liking and wanting were generated by entirely separate neural populations, VP units that fire to palatability at receipt should not also be the units that fire to predictive cues; Smith 2011 shows they are.

If drive amplification (sodium depletion) drove approach directly without routing through hedonic recoding, the cue-evoked VP firing pattern should change immediately on the deficit signal alone; the data show the cue firing pattern updates only after the reincoded palatability has been experienced.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Single neurons in ventral pallidum encode both hedonic palatability (firing at oral receipt) and incentive salience (firing to predictive cues). When physiological state changes from sated to sodium-depleted, the palatability-coded firing to a previously aversive saline taste flips from low to high; the cue-evoked firing then follows. The cue's incentive value is updated by the experienced palatability, not by the deficit signal alone.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-295 says drive amplification on its own (SD-012 turning up drive_level) does not produce approach behaviour -- it must propagate through liking-stream activation (the appetitive valence stream) which then supplies the cue-side pull for approach action selection. Smith, Berridge & Aldridge 2011 supply the cleanest single-cell mechanistic version of this claim that exists in the literature. The drive change (sodium depletion) does NOT directly rewire the cue-evoked motivational firing. It first changes the palatability code at receipt; the experienced (recoded) palatability is then what reshapes the cue-evoked firing on subsequent trials. This is the biological version of MECH-295's necessity link: drive -> liking-recoding -> cue-driven approach.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper uses a sodium-depletion / salt-appetite paradigm where the substrate manipulation is physiological (homeostatic state) and the read-out is a single VP firing pattern. MECH-295 is about an artificial agent's approach_commit metric in EXQ-483. The liking-as-link logic transfers cleanly, but the timescale (single trials over minutes in rats) and the architectural granularity (one neural population vs separate streams in REE) differ. The paper also does not directly block VP hedonic coding to test whether wanting fails; it shows that wanting tracks experienced palatability, which is necessity by correlation across drive state, not by ablation.
mapping.source_context: Adult rats, chronic single-unit recording in VP, intraoral taste delivery (sucrose, saline, water), Pavlovian cue conditioning, sodium-depletion-induced salt appetite shift, within-subject pre/post-deficit comparison.

confidence_components.notes: PNAS, well-controlled within-subject design, single-cell resolution. Mapping fidelity is the highest of the entries in this directory: the paper specifically dissects whether drive change rewires cue-firing directly or whether it must route through palatability recoding, and the answer is the second. Transfer risk moderate -- VP firing to MECH-295's z_liking activation is an architectural translation, but biologically grounded.
